text.title: ES Versioning Worked Example
text.summary: Worked example: upcasting OrderPlaced v1 → v2 (rename customer_id → buyer_id).

example.title: OrderPlaced v1 → v2
example.code:
v1 fields: - order_id: UUID - customer_id: UUID - total_cents: int
v2 fields (rename customer_id to buyer_id; same semantic): - order_id: UUID - buyer_id: UUID - total_cents: int
Upcaster (faion/events/upcasters/order_placed_v1_to_v2.py):
def upcast(v1: dict) -> dict: return { "order_id": v1["order_id"], "buyer_id": v1["customer_id"], "total_cents": v1["total_cents"], "event_version": 2, }
Catalog entry: OrderPlaced: versions: - version: 1 deprecated: true fields: [order_id, customer_id, total_cents] - version: 2 upcaster_from: 1 upcaster_module: faion.events.upcasters.order_placed_v1_to_v2 fields: [order_id, buyer_id, total_cents]
example.note: Reader receives `OrderPlaced` always at the latest version; v1 events are upcast on the fly.

example.title: Rename without upcaster
example.code: Engineer renames customer_id to buyer_id directly in the v1 class. No version bump. No upcaster. Deploy reads a stream with mix of pre- and post-rename events. Deserializer raises KeyError on either old or new field name.
example.note: Production outage. Roll back, then follow the good-path procedure.
